basics.name: Quyen V. Vu
basics.label: Postdoctoral Scholar
basics.summary: Computational biophysicist studying protein folding, misfolding, and topological entanglement using molecular dynamics simulations and statistical-mechanical models.

work.name: Department of Chemistry, Penn State University
work.summary: Investigate molecular mechanisms of protein folding, misfolding, and non-covalent lasso entanglement using atomistic and coarse-grained molecular simulations.

work.name: Institute of Physics, Polish Academy of Sciences, Warsaw, Poland
work.summary: Developed and applied coarse-grained and all-atom simulation models to study co-translational protein folding on the ribosome.

highlights: Journal of the American Chemical Society

references.name: Prof. Mai Suan Li
references.reference: Prof. Mai Suan Li is a professor of computational biophysics at the Institute of Physics, Polish Academy of Sciences, Warsaw, Poland, with expertise in molecular simulation and biomolecular physics.

references.name: Prof. Edward P. O'Brien
references.reference: Prof. Edward P. O'Brien is a professor of chemistry at Penn State University, with expertise in computational biophysics, protein folding, and ribosome-associated molecular processes.

projects.name: Protein entanglement, misfolding, and aging
projects.summary: Investigate whether native non-covalent lasso entanglements create structural liabilities that promote misfolding and contribute to age-associated proteome remodeling in yeast.

projects.name: Molecular mechanisms of entanglement-mediated protein misfolding and disease
projects.summary: Use molecular simulations to characterize how native topological entanglements alter folding pathways, stabilize misfolded states, and shape disease-relevant protein behavior.

projects.name: Hydrophobic effect in the ribosome vestibule
projects.summary: Quantified how water ordering in the ribosome vestibule weakens hydrophobic driving forces that can influence nascent-chain folding.

projects.name: Electrostatic interactions govern extreme nascent protein ejection times from ribosomes and can delay ribosome recycling in E. coli
projects.summary: Studied how electrostatic interactions modulate nascent-protein ejection from the ribosome and can delay ribosome recycling in E. coli.

presentations.title: The driving force for co-translational protein folding is weaker in the ribosome vestibule due to greater water ordering

presentations.title: A new class of protein misfolding revealed by all-atom simulations and supported by experiments
